rationale: In the conceptual framework, the citing paper explicitly uses Lemon and Nowlis (2002) as evidence that product positioning moderates marketing-mix effectiveness. That prior result is used to justify the paper's own core setup of interacting ad content with price and quality when modeling search and sales effects. This is therefore more than a passing background mention, even though the cited paper's specific methods are not reused.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Lemon and Nowlis (2002) as conceptual groundwork showing that price/quality positioning can change marketing response (e.g., display and feature effects). It builds on that premise to frame and test positioning-contingent effects of informational and emotional TV ad content.
